_survey: T-primitive-survey 2026-03-24g: completeness survey against 3 target programs.

primitives.description: List all files in ctx[dir] and write sorted JSON path list to ctx[files].
Pop the first path from ctx[files] into ctx[current_file]; write remaining list back to ctx[files].
Check if ctx[files] is exhausted; write ctx[done]='true' or 'false'.
Stat ctx[current_file]; write ISO mtime to ctx[file_mtime] and byte size to ctx[file_size].
Read first N lines (ctx[read_head_lines], default 40) of ctx[current_file]; write to ctx[content].
Detect file type of ctx[current_file] from extension and magic bytes; write to ctx[file_type].
Append ctx[list_value] to persistent named list ctx[list_name] (D095 lists table); write updated count to ctx[list_count].
Remove oldest item (FIFO) from persistent named list ctx[list_name]; write item to ctx[list_value], key to ctx[list_key], remaining count to ctx[list_count].
Count items in persistent named list ctx[list_name]; write count to ctx[list_count].

primitives.notes: node_id_key and node_parent optional. node_parent defaults to CP1.
primitives.description: Create or upsert a Memory node from ctx[node_narrative] + ctx[node_type]; link under ctx[node_parent] (default CP1); write id to ctx[node_id].
Link ctx[link_parent] → ctx[link_child] in the memory graph. Idempotent.

primitives.notes: search_limit optional; default 5. ctx[node_id] set to top result id.
primitives.description: Search cortex.search() with ctx[search_query]; write newline-joined 'id|type|narrative' list to ctx[search_results], count to ctx[search_count], top id to ctx[node_id].
Push ctx[twm_content] as a TWM observation; write obs id to ctx[twm_obs_id]. Use for salience signals, alerts, work-in-progress observations.
Read active TWM observations; write newline-joined summary to ctx[twm_items] and count to ctx[twm_count].

primitives.notes: split_sep defaults to newline. split_maxn defaults to 0 (unlimited).
primitives.description: Split ctx[content] on ctx[split_sep]; write JSON list to ctx[split_parts] and count to ctx[split_count].
Regex-search ctx[content] with ctx[regex_pattern]; write first match to ctx[regex_match], 'true'/'false' to ctx[regex_matched].
String-interpolate ctx[format_template] substituting {key} placeholders from context; write result to ctx[format_result]. Missing keys left as-is.

primitives.notes: slice_start defaults to 0. slice_end defaults to end of string. Negatives count from end.
primitives.description: Slice ctx[content][slice_start:slice_end]; write to ctx[slice_result]. Useful for truncation.
Write a literal string value into ctx[key]. Both key and value come from the step dict.
Conditional jump. Reads ctx[if.key], applies op, jumps to goto_true or goto_false step number.
Unconditional jump to the given step number.

missing.description: Filter ctx[files] JSON path list by extension or type (ctx[filter_type]); write filtered list back to ctx[files].
missing.reason: Schema habits need to process only certain file types (e.g. only .epub files) without iterating and discarding.

missing.description: Write ctx[log_message] to ring_memory with category='schema'. Structured trace for debugging without side-effecting the response.
missing.reason: Schema habits need lightweight tracing. prim_twm_push is wrong tool (TWM is for salience signals, not debug traces).

missing.description: Post ctx[cc_message] to the shared CC channel as ctx[cc_author] (default 'igor'); write 'true'/'false' to ctx[cc_ok].
missing.reason: Both worker monitor and design loop need to write to the channel without LLM overhead. channel.py post() is one HTTP call — trivial wrapper.

missing.description: Read last ctx[cc_limit] (default 10) messages from CC channel; write newline-joined 'author: text' to ctx[cc_messages] and count to ctx[cc_count].
missing.reason: Design loop habit needs to inspect channel state without an LLM call.

missing.description: List jobs from job_manager; ctx[job_status_filter] = 'active'|'all' (default 'active'); write newline-joined 'id|status|title' to ctx[job_list] and count to ctx[job_count].
missing.reason: Worker monitor needs to enumerate job state without LLM. job_manager.active_count() + list is one Python call.

missing.description: Get status of ctx[job_id]; write status string to ctx[job_status], progress pct to ctx[job_progress], summary to ctx[job_summary].
missing.reason: Worker monitor needs per-job status checks for threshold-based TWM alerts.

missing.description: List registered daemons from DaemonSupervisor; write newline-joined 'name|status|last_ping' to ctx[daemon_list] and count to ctx[daemon_count].
missing.reason: Worker monitor habit needs to see all registered daemons without calling into NE.

missing.description: Check health of ctx[daemon_name] via DaemonSupervisor; write 'true'/'false' to ctx[daemon_ok] and last ping ISO timestamp to ctx[daemon_last_ping].
missing.reason: Per-daemon health check allows targeted alerts rather than full scan.

missing.description: Call get_machine_availability(); write formatted report to ctx[machines_report].
missing.reason: Worker monitor can include cluster availability in its summary without an LLM call.

missing.description: Store ctx[decision_text] as a design decision via decision_manager; ctx[decision_id] optional (auto-generated); write 'true'/'false' to ctx[decision_ok].
missing.reason: Design loop habit should be able to persist decisions directly without routing through NE.

missing.description: Search Calibre library for ctx[book_query]; write newline-joined 'title|author|format|path' to ctx[book_list] and count to ctx[book_count].
missing.reason: Book reader habit needs to locate books without an LLM call. ebook_reader.find_book() exists — one-line wrapper.

missing.description: Open book at ctx[book_path] via ebook_reader.open_book(); write handle key to ctx[book_handle], title to ctx[book_title], sentence count to ctx[book_sentence_count].
missing.reason: Book reader habit needs to open books and track handle across steps.

missing.description: Read next ctx[book_chunk_n] (default 1) sentences from ctx[book_handle]; write text to ctx[content], current position to ctx[book_position]. Advances internal reading state.
missing.reason: Book reader habit needs to iterate through book content chunk-by-chunk. ebook_reader.read_chunk() exists.

missing.description: Feed ctx[content] into the word graph (tokenize → bigram extraction → weight update); write new node count to ctx[wg_nodes_added] and edge count to ctx[wg_edges_added].
missing.reason: The whole point of the book reader as a schema habit is autonomous ingestion into the word graph. This is the core learning primitive. No existing primitive covers it — word_graph.train_on_text() needs a wrapper with forensic logging and progress tracking.

missing.description: Update a field (trigger, metadata key, inertia) on habit ctx[habit_id]; write 'true'/'false' to ctx[habit_ok].
missing.reason: Design loop habit needs to self-modify habits (calibration) without going through the LLM. Requires safe write path — habit self-edit is high-inertia and needs its own gate, not just a cortex.store() wrapper.

missing.description: Run thalamus intent/tone/complexity classification on ctx[content] (zero-inference: graph traversal only); write results to ctx[parsed_intent], ctx[parsed_tone], ctx[parsed_complexity].
missing.reason: Design loop habit needs to classify incoming CC messages to route them without an LLM call. Exposes thalamus as a primitive — requires adding a non-NE code path through thalamus.classify_standalone().

survey._note: T-primitive-survey 2026-03-24g. For each target program: step enumeration + primitive classification.

book_reader.description: Autonomous ebook ingestion: find book → open → read chunks → ingest to word graph → store memory nodes → loop.

worker_monitor.description: Periodic daemon / job health check: scan jobs → scan daemons → check machines → TWM-push any alerts → post summary to channel.

cc_design_loop.description: CC channel monitoring: read messages → classify intent → route to decision store or habit update → post acknowledgement.

gap_ranking._note: Gaps ranked by how many target programs need them. Implement in this order.

catalog_debt._note: These primitives exist in os_primitives.py but were missing from catalog v1. Now documented above.
